questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding 'Gene-Drive Technology': 1. It is a genetic engineering technique that alters normal Mendelian inheritance patterns. 2. It decreases the likelihood of a specific gene being passed onto the next generation to eliminate mutations. 3. It can be utilized to control agricultural pests and vector-borne diseases. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Gene-Drive Technology (GDT) is a genetic engineering technique that modifies genes to alter normal Mendelian inheritance. Statement 2 is incorrect: Rather than decreasing the likelihood, GDT increases the likelihood that a particular suite of genes will be passed onto the next generation, allowing a trait to spread rapidly through a population. Statement 3 is correct: GDT can be used to stop insects from spreading vector-borne diseases (like mosquitoes) and to control agricultural pests.
questions.takeaway: Gene-Drive Technology bypasses traditional Mendelian inheritance rules to ensure a specific modified gene is passed to almost all offspring, making it a powerful tool for controlling pest populations and vector-borne diseases.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Gene-Drive Technology (GDT) can stop insects from spreading vector-borne diseases and pests. It is a genetic engineering technique that modifies genes to alter normal Mendelian inheritance, increasing the likelihood that a particular suite of genes will be passed onto the next generation.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Which one of the following statements best describes the core mechanism of 'Gene-Drive Technology'?

options.text: It involves the introduction of sterile male insects to naturally crash the population of disease vectors without altering their genome.
It modifies genes to alter normal inheritance, ensuring a specific trait spreads rapidly through a population.
It strictly adheres to Mendelian inheritance principles to preserve the genetic diversity of endangered species.
It is a traditional selective breeding method used to enhance the yield of pest-resistant agricultural crops.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct. Gene-Drive Technology is a genetic engineering technique that alters normal Mendelian inheritance, increasing the likelihood that a particular suite of genes will be passed onto the next generation. Option A describes the Sterile Insect Technique (SIT), not Gene-Drive. Option C is incorrect because GDT explicitly alters or bypasses normal Mendelian inheritance. Option D is incorrect as GDT is a genetic engineering technique, not traditional selective breeding.
questions.takeaway: The defining feature of Gene-Drive Technology is its ability to 'drive' a specific gene through a population by altering standard Mendelian inheritance probabilities.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It is a genetic engineering technique that modifies genes to alter normal Mendelian inheritance, increasing the likelihood that a particular suite of genes will be passed onto the next generation.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: If a genetic trait designed to control agricultural pests is introduced into a wild population using Gene-Drive Technology (GDT), which of the following best describes its expected inheritance pattern compared to natural reproduction?

options.text: The trait will spread through the population at a rate higher than predicted by standard Mendelian genetics.
The trait will be inherited by exactly half of the offspring in each generation, preserving natural genetic equilibrium.
The trait will decrease in frequency over successive generations to prevent ecological imbalance.
The trait will only be expressed in the initially modified individuals and cannot be passed onto the next generation.

questions.explanation: Gene-Drive Technology is specifically designed to alter normal Mendelian inheritance. Under normal Mendelian genetics, a gene has a 50% chance of being passed on (Option B). GDT increases the likelihood that a particular suite of genes will be passed onto the next generation, meaning it spreads at a much higher rate than standard Mendelian genetics would predict.
questions.takeaway: The defining feature of Gene-Drive Technology is its ability to 'drive' a gene through a population by ensuring it is inherited by more than the standard 50% of offspring, breaking Mendelian rules.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It is a genetic engineering technique that modifies genes to alter normal Mendelian inheritance, increasing the likelihood that a particular suite of genes will be passed onto the next generation.
